datasets.name: Child Labor Report (2016 to 2022)
datasets.description: This dataset contains information on child labor and forced labor worldwide from ILAB's three flagship reports: Findings on the Worst Forms of Child Labor; List of Goods Produced by Child Labor or Forced Labor; and List of Products Produced by Forced or Indentured Child Labor. This table contains data from the 2016 - 2022 reporting cycles. For more information, see ILAB's International Child Labor and Forced Labor Reports page. Please cite USDOL when using Child Labor Report data.

datasets.name: ImportWatch_Goods_HS
datasets.description: ImportWatch datasets match essential information on foreign labor abuses from ILAB's three flagship reports (Findings on the Worst Forms of Child Labor; List of Goods Produced by Child Labor or Forced Labor; and List of Products Produced by Forced or Indentured Child Labor) with U.S. import trade data, including Harmonized Tariff Schedule Codes, to empower users to identify high-risk imports, avoid penalties, and strengthen compliance with U.S. labor standards. This helps ensure that unfair trade practices abroad do not undercut compliant firms at home, stifle U.S. economic growth, or put American businesses at a disadvantage. There are 3 tables combining data from ILAB's essential reporting with U.S. import trade data. For more information, see ILAB's reports page (https://www.dol.gov/agencies/ilab/resources/reports/child-labor) and Import Watch page (https://www.dol.gov/agencies/ilab/importwatch). Please cite USDOL when using ImportWatch data.

datasets.name: LaborShield_ReportingData
datasets.description: LaborShield datasets empower companies to assess their supply chains, flag risks early, and act before they escalate into costly operational disruptions, reputational damage, or legal challenges. These datasets contain information on foreign labor abuses from ILAB's three flagship reports: Findings on the Worst Forms of Child Labor; List of Goods Produced by Child Labor or Forced Labor; and List of Products Produced by Forced or Indentured Child Labor. This information is also available in ILAB's app, LaborShield. For more information, see ILAB's reports page (https://www.dol.gov/agencies/ilab/resources/reports/child-labor). Please cite USDOL when using LaborShield data.

datasets.name: National Database of Childcare Prices (NDCP)
datasets.description: *UPDATED* The National Database of Childcare Prices (NDCP) is the most comprehensive federal source of childcare prices at the county level. The database offers childcare price data by childcare provider type, age of children, and county characteristics. Data are available from 2008 to 2022. The technical guide and more information can be found on our website: https://www.dol.gov/agencies/wb/topics/featured-childcare

datasets.name: Accident
datasets.description: This dataset contains information on all accidents, injuries and illnesses reported by mine operators and contractors beginning on 1/1/1983. The data in the table is obtained from the Mine Accident, Injury and Illness Report form (MSHA Form 7000-1). It provides information about the accident/injury/illness such as type, mine location, lost days and the degree of injury. Document number is the unique key for this data.

datasets.name: Address of Records - Mines
datasets.description:
This dataset contains mine addresses for all mines in the database except for Abandoned mines prior to 1998 from MSHA's legacy system. The address is the one entered by the mine operator at the time a Legal ID form is completed for a requested mine identification number. It is updated by MSHA district offices as required. The Mine ID is the unique key for this data.
(Includes Abandoned or Abandoned and Sealed Mines)
Note: Address of Record is not necessarily the same as Mine Location Address.

datasets.name: Assessed Violations
datasets.description:
This dataset contains information about violations that have been assessed penalties by MSHA, and issued on or after 1/1/1995. 
Other qualifications:  Activity_Codes  NOT IN ('09',  '11',  '14',  'AGA',   'AGB', 'AGC',   'BBC',   'DGA',   'DGB',  'DGC', 'EAC',   'EAP',   'EGA',   'EHA',   'EJA',   'EKA', 'ELA',  'E11',     'E12',     'T02',     'T13'   ); Violator_Type_cd IN ( 'Contractor', 'Operator' ); type_of_term <> 'Vacated'.
Some of the values available are Citation Number; Violator ID; Violator Name; Proposed Penalty Amount; Current Assessment Amount; Paid Proposed Penalty Amount; Bill Print Date; Final Order Date; Last Action Code; Last Action Date.
Note: This dataset's qualifications and/or update schedule may not match exactly the qualifications an/or update schedules of other MSHA datasets and therefore data produced by this dataset might differ from data produced by other MSHA datasets.

datasets.name: Contractor Name ID Lookup
datasets.description: The dataset lists all MSHA registered mining contractors. The data fields present are: Contractor Name; and Contractor ID.

datasets.name: Contractor Employment / Production (Annual)
datasets.description: This dataset contains the annual summation of employee hours and coal production reported by contractors where the average quarterly employment is greater than zero with grouping by calendar year, subunit code and mine ID beginning on 1/1/1983. The subunit code identifies the location or operation of the mine relating to the: (01) Underground; (02) Surface at underground; (03) Strip, quarry, open pit; (04) Auger; (05) Culm bank/refuse pile; (06) Dredge; (12) Other mining; (17) Independent shops or yards; (30) Mill operation/preparation plant; (99) Office workers at mine site.  (MNM Operators are not required to report production.)

datasets.name: Controller History
datasets.description: This dataset shows the history of controllers at mining operators.

datasets.name: Commodity Lookup
datasets.description: This dataset contains list of MSHA Commodities and Commodity Codes (SIC; NAICS).

datasets.name: Operator Employment / Production (Annual)
datasets.description: This dataset contains the annual summation of employee hours and coal production reported by mine operators where the average quarterly employment is greater than zero with grouping by calendar year, subunit code and mine ID beginning on 1/1/1983. The subunit code identifies the location or operation of the mine relating to the: (01) Underground; (02) Surface at underground; (03) Strip, quarry, open pit; (04) Auger; (05) Culm bank/refuse pile; (06) Dredge; (12) Other mining; (17) Independent shops or yards; (30) Mill operation/preparation plant; (99) Office workers at mine site. (MNM Operators are not required to report production.)

datasets.name: Inspection
datasets.description:
The Mine Inspections dataset lists every mine inspection conducted by MSHA beginning on 1/1/1983. The data includes the name of the mine inspected, MSHA's internal inspection number (event number), the type of inspection and the start and end dates of the inspection. Event Number is the unique key for this dataset. It also includes the Mine ID associated with the inspections which can be used to relate it to the Mines Data Set.
Other qualifications used:  acty_cd  NOT IN 09, 11, 14, AGA, AGB, AGC, BBC, DGA, DGB, DGC, EAC, EAP, EGA, EHA, EJA, EKA, ELA, E11, E12, T02,T13.
Note: This dataset's qualifications and/or update schedule may not match exactly the qualifications an/or update schedules of other MSHA datasets and therefore data produced by this dataset might differ from data produced by other MSHA datasets.

datasets.name: Mines
datasets.description:
The Mine dataset lists all Coal and Metal/Non-Metal mines under MSHA's jurisdiction since 1/1/1970. It includes such information as the current status of each mine (Active, Abandoned, NonProducing, etc.), the current owner and operating company, commodity codes and physical attributes of the mine. Mine ID is the unique key for this data.
(Includes Abandoned or Abandoned and Sealed Mines)

datasets.name: Contractor History at Mines
datasets.description: This dataset contains Contractor history at Mines. It shows Contractor ID, Contractor Name, Mine ID, Contractor Start Date at the Mine and Contractor End Date at the Mine.

datasets.name: Operator History at Mines
datasets.description: This dataset contains Operator history at Mines. It shows Operator ID, Operator Name, Mine ID, Operator Start Date at the Mine and Operator End Date at the Mine.

datasets.name: Contractor Employment / Production (Quarterly)
datasets.description: This dataset contains employment and coal production reported by contractors for each quarter in a calendar year, by subunit and mine ID, starting with 1983. The subunit code identifies the location or operation of the mine relating to the: (01) Underground; (02) Surface at underground; (03) Strip, quarry, open pit; (04) Auger; (05) Culm bank/refuse pile; (06) Dredge; (12) Other mining; (17) Independent shops or yards; (30) Mill operation/preparation plant; (99) Office workers at mine site.  (MNM Operators are not required to report production.)

datasets.name: Operator Employment / Production (Quarterly)
datasets.description: This dataset contains employment and coal production reported by mine operators for each quarter in a calendar year, by subunit and mine ID beginning on 1/1/1983. The subunit code identifies the location or operation of the mine relating to the: (01) Underground; (02) Surface at underground; (03) Strip, quarry, open pit; (04) Auger; (05) Culm bank/refuse pile; (06) Dredge; (12) Other mining; (17) Independent shops or yards; (30) Mill operation/preparation plant; (99) Office workers at mine site. (MNM Operators are not required to report production.)

datasets.name: Violation
datasets.description:
This dataset contains violations issued as a result of MSHA enforcement, during inspections. Each inspection results in a unique Event Number that can be associated with other violations and is a direct link to the Inspections Data Set. Violations that are assessed penalties in the later process can be also found in the Assessed Violations Dataset. The violator name at the time of the violation, the actual date the violation occurred and the date the violation was issued are in this file. This table contains details about the specific citation/order/safeguard issued, such as Section of the Act, CFR standard, relevant dates and the conditions or practices which caused and constituted a violation or an imminent danger. The data is captured from (MSHA form 7000-3).
This dataset does not have a date limitation; excludes Vacated violations; and pertains only to Operator and Contractor violations.
Other qualifications used:  acty_cd  NOT IN 09, 11, 14, AGA, AGB, AGC, BBC, DGA, DGB, DGC, EAC, EAP, EGA, EHA, EJA, EKA, ELA, E11, E12, T02,T13.
Note: This dataset's qualifications and/or update schedule may not match exactly the qualifications an/or update schedules of other MSHA datasets and therefore data produced by this dataset might differ from data produced by other MSHA datasets.

datasets.name: Enforcement
datasets.description:
The dataset contains all concluded WHD compliance actions since FY 2005. The dataset includes whether any violations were found and the back wage amount, number of employees due back wages, and civil money penalties assessed.

NOTE: Findings Start Date and Findings End Date are not equal to Case Open Date and Case Close Date, which are not included in the dataset.

datasets.name: Office of the Chief Accountant Case Tracking System (OCATS)
datasets.description: The dataset consists of closed cases that resulted in penalty assessments by EBSA since 2000. This data provides information on EBSA's enforcement programs to enforce ERISA's Form 5500 Annual Return/Report filing requirement focusing on deficient filers, late filers and non-filers.

datasets.name: Accident Lookup2
datasets.description:
This lookup table provides a listing of the codes used in the osha_accident and osha_accident_injury datasets.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Accident
datasets.description:
This dataset provides information regarding incidents reported by employers and investigated by OSHA.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Accident Abstract
datasets.description:
This dataset provides a brief narrative describing the incident reported.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Accident Injury
datasets.description:
This dataset provides information about incidents reported to OSHA, such as information about the circumstances of the incident and about the injured person. Field completeness varies because of changes in data entry, technology, and policies. Refer to the Accident Lookup2 Dataset for Code Associations.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Inspection
datasets.description:
This dataset provides the inspection case detail for OSHA inspections. The dataset includes information about why the inspection was conducted.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Optional Code Info
datasets.description:
This dataset provides optional information collected for OSHA inspections.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Related Activity
datasets.description:
This dataset provides information on activities (Complaints, Referrals, Accidents and Inspections) related to OSHA inspections.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Emphasis Codes
datasets.description:
This dataset provides emphasis program codes related to OSHA inspections, such as national, strategic, local and state emphasis programs.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Violation
datasets.description:
This dataset provides details about violations and penalty assessments resulting from OSHA inspections, including the type and seriousness of the violations.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Violation Event
datasets.description:
This dataset provides details regarding the current status OSHA violations, including penalty, abatement and settlement.

Additional records can be viewed and/or downloaded from OSHA’s website at www.osha.gov/data.

datasets.name: Violation Gen Duty Std
datasets.description: This dataset provides information about citations of OSHA's general duty standard (5a1) including narrative text.

datasets.name: OTAA Petition
datasets.description:
The Trade Adjustment Assistance (TAA) Program sought to provide adversely affected workers with opportunities to obtain the skills, credentials, resources, and support necessary to (re)build skills for future jobs. Any member of a worker group certified by the Department could be eligible to receive the following benefits and services at a local American Job Center: training, employment and case management services, job search allowances, relocation allowances, and income support in the form of Trade Readjustment Allowances (TRA).

Please note: On July 1, 2022, the termination provision under Section 285(a) of the Trade Act of 1974, as amended, took effect. Until further notice, the Department may not issue any determinations and may not accept any new petitions or requests for reconsideration, and as a result, there will not be meaningful updates to this data set.

datasets.name: Apprenticeship Participant Characteristics and Training Outcomes
datasets.description: This dataset contains information across the apprenticeship participation lifecycle, describing characteristics, activities and outcomes of new, active, and exiting apprentices.  Data are available for age cohorts, gender, race, veteran status, and education demographics.  Dataset also includes state data from the registered apprenticeship partners, providing key statistics on apprentices by fiscal year.

datasets.name: VETS 4212 Federal Contractor Reporting
datasets.description:
This dataset contains required veteran employment data by federal contractors/subcontractors, who are required to report annually the number of employees in their workforces who are veterans covered under Vietnam Era Veterans' Readjustment Assistance Act (VEVRAA).

The U.S. Department of Labor's Veterans' Employment and Training Service (VETS) and Office of Federal Contractor Compliance Programs (OFCCP) have supported affirmative actions to employ and advance in employment of covered veterans since 2008. As legislatively mandated under 38 U.S. Code Section 4212, codified at 41 CFR 61-300, contractors and subcontractors who enter into, or modify a contract or subcontract with the federal government, and whose contract meets the criteria set forth in the above legislation / regulations, are required to report annually on their affirmative action efforts in employing veterans. Data reported through form VETS-4212 is used by OFCCP in compliance evaluations.

datasets.name: Unemployment Insurance National Weekly Claims
datasets.description: --NOTE: IF YOU NEED LOW LATENCY ACCESS TO THIS DATA, PLEASE CONTINUE TO ACCESS IT THROUGH https://www.dol.gov/ui/data.pdf. UPDATES TO THESE DATA LAG PUBLICATION BY NO LESS THAN 30 MINUTES AND ARE OFFERED AS AN ADDITIONAL MODE OF ACCESS-- Weekly unemployment insurance (UI) claims are reported by each state's Unemployment Insurance program offices. These claims may be used for monitoring workload volume, assessing state program operations, and for assessing labor market conditions. States initially report claims directly taken by the state liable for the benefit payments, regardless of where the claimant who filed the claim resided. These are the basis for the advance initial claims and continued claims reported each week. The following week initial claims and continued claims are revised based on a second reporting by states that reflect the claimants by state of residence. Initial claims are filed by an unemployed individual after a separation from an employer. The claimant requests a determination of basic eligibility for the UI program. The count of U.S. initial claims for unemployment insurance is a leading economic indicator because it is an indication of emerging labor market conditions in the country. However, these are weekly administrative data which are difficult to seasonally adjust, making the series subject to some volatility. A person who has already filed an initial claim and who has experienced a week of unemployment then files a continued claim to claim benefits for that week of unemployment. On a weekly basis, continued claims (are also referred to as insured unemployment), reflect a good approximation of the current number of insured unemployed workers filing for UI benefits. The count of U.S. continued weeks claimed is also a good indicator of labor market conditions. While continued claims are not a leading indicator (they roughly coincide with economic cycles at their peaks and lag at cycle troughs), they provide confirming evidence of the direction of the U.S. economy.

datasets.name: Unemployment Insurance State Weekly Claims
datasets.description: --NOTE: IF YOU NEED LOW LATENCY ACCESS TO THIS DATA, PLEASE CONTINUE TO ACCESS IT THROUGH https://www.dol.gov/ui/data.pdf. UPDATES TO THESE DATA LAG PUBLICATION BY NO LESS THAN 30 MINUTES AND ARE OFFERED AS AN ADDITIONAL MODE OF ACCESS-- Unemployment Insurance (UI) claims data contained on the ETA 539 report are used in current economic analysis of unemployment trends in the Nation, and in each state. Initial claims measure emerging unemployment and continued weeks claimed measure the number of the insured unemployed. These data are published in ETA´s weekly release "Unemployment Insurance Claims." The comments on claims activity are used to explain the changes in the levels of the data reported from week to week.

datasets.name: Training Dataset (Industries)
datasets.description: This is a training dataset featured in the “Conditional Filtering” section of our API User Guide. It was created to help API users practice writing a json formatted string for the “filter_object” parameter, which allows users to subset a dataset based on conditions for one or more fields. Practice filtering this dataset using the example filter conditions from the User Guide or try your own.
